subject: PhD Analysis
instructions: State the following theorems in problems 1–8. Prove one of the theorems in problem 3 or 7. Solve problems 9–13, where \((X,\Sigma,\mu)\) is a measure space and \(F\) is a Banach space. Write complete computations and explanations. Do not use arrows or other unnecessary signs. Use words for explanations. Write complete sentences.

problems.text: If \(f_n\to f\) in \(L_F^2(\mu)\) and \(f_n\to g\), \(\mu\)-a.e., then \(f=g\), \(\mu\)-a.e.
Let \(\mathcal F\subset\Sigma\) be a σ-algebra. Prove the existence of the conditional expectation \(E(f/\mathcal F)\) for \(f\in L_F^2(\mu)\).
Let \(\mathcal R\) be a ring generating \(\Sigma\) and \(f\in L_F^1(\mu)\). Prove that if \(\int_A f\,d\mu=0\) for every \(A\in\mathcal R\), then \(f=0\), \(\mu\)-a.e.

Let \((X,\mathcal F)\) and \((Y,\mathcal J)\) be measurable spaces, \(\mu:\mathcal F	o\mathbb R\) and \(
u:\mathcal J	o\mathbb R\), σ-additive measures. Prove the existence of the product-measure \(\mu	imes
u:\mathcal F	imes\mathcal J	o\mathbb R\) satisfying \(\mu	imes
u(A	imes B)=\mu(A)
u(B)\) for \(A	imes B\in\mathcal F	imes\mathcal J\) and \(|\mu	imes
u|=|\mu|	imes|
u|\).
If \(f\in L_F^p(\mu)\), there is a sequence \((f_n)\) of \(F\)-step functions
